title: The stereotyped romantic plunge
wikitext:
[]: The stereotyped "romantic" plunge.             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Fri Dec 27, 2013 6:29 pm I know this question is trivial and "google it"-typed as hell, but I just can't find any kind of Information. Everybody knows this completely clichéd scene that appears nowadays in nearly every slightly romantic anime: One person ,of the pair with sexual or romantic tensions, stumbles or slips and lands on top of his/her "significant other", causing irritated behavior on both sides. My, possibly completely stupid, question: Was this type of scene "invented" by NGE ? / Was the Rei/Shinji-Apartment-scene the first of its kind? If there not already some kind of thread approaching this topic, that I couldn't find, we could morph this thread in a "Things EVA invented"-Thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Dec 27, 2013 7:03 pm Usually it's the male falling on the female character, but hell no.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Fri Dec 27, 2013 7:23 pm The earliest example on http://tvtropes.org/pmwiki/pmwiki.php/Main/ThanksForTheMammary is Ranma 1/2 - which is the late 1980s, isn't it?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 28, 2013 3:06 am The whole of the Rei's apartment scene is playing on the established notions of the walking in on a girl scene; unlike her contemporaries, Rei doesn't reach into hammerspace to give Shinji a pounding.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 28, 2013 1:34 pm Yeah - it's very much relying on people having these expectations in their head and being bewildered when none of that happens/focussed on the unusual reaction on Rei's part. You see this all the time, walking on some female naked = instant love interest. Which I guess can be just a vaguely titilating way to create comic relief, and doesn't always come with unfortunate implications, but there can be. Like the idea that the chick in question could somehow be "bonded" to the guy because she lost her "ogle-virginity" an and now must marry him or something since she already "gave something away" or "invested" in him... with no actual participation of her own, somehow. Also, RL girls rarely like to be looked at, allthough they typically would probably be more embarassed than irrationally trigger-happy. If it really was accidental, that is - Someone who doesn't respect you as a person obviously makes rather poor boyfriend material, allthough the generic protagonist typically matures out of this with time. In any case, Anno wanted to subvert that to hell. Like anything else. Unlike his fellow shonen protagonists, just walking on Rei naked didn't earn Shinji any points with her. No, he had to a) show some interest about her as a person and talk about her motivations (and later show he actually remembered what she was sprouting) and b) work together with her to defeat a scary monster he was thoroughly terrified of because it nearly killed him earlier that day. And for bonus fun, Rei even gets a good look at nekkid Shinji, not that she really minds either time. Another point that probably was to be made here is that it would be seriously weird if, as we see in the occasional anime/typical for comic relief, everything was fine by the next scene, like nothing happened. Poor Shinji doesn't have a clue how to talk to her afterwards, he can't really gauge just how pissed (if at all) she is.... and there's obviously the whole "She doesn't mind because she was grown in a vat and generally treated like an object to be used for most of her life, and that's terrible" angle. One must consider, tho, that straight versions of the whole thing regularly occur with Asuka, although the subversion here might be that the only reacts in the classic fashion because she thinks (or is desperately trying to convince herself) that the world revolves around her, disregarding the obvious sighns that most of the time, it really was accidental and Shinji was, for example, wondering what she was doing since there's an angel at large that could sink the ship they're on at any given moment. There's also the similar boob-gag with Mari, where, once again, Shinji is the only one who minds, because Mari doesn't have any sense of personal space, and the encounter, much like the one with Rei, becomes more of a tool of characterization than an actual romance comedy scene. The rehersal in Q is probably also worth mentioning - much like ep 23 reenacts the "first meeting in the hallway" we get this, where Shinji, already used to Rei's lack of modesty, asks her to get dressed before coming in (albeit a bit frantically, since he IS still a (technical) teenager and not immune to her charms at all), which could be seen as a show of respect for her as a human. (Especially when you recal how Ritsuko & Gendo just have her stand around naked like some appliance to be poked and prodded) All of this, of course, goes way over ReiQs head, who just hears, "I command thee to denudify thyself!", and any fun value is quickly quelled by the ensuing, heartwrenching scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Sat Dec 28, 2013 2:19 pm ^ I already expected somethig like that, since the two things Anno's doing with EVA is 1. producing stereotypes (Rei is now a archetype) 2. reversing stereotypes (e.g. The EVAs not being robots or the development of the plot; starting like a typical mecha-show and ending as a complex, multilayered, demanding drama with unbelievable deep characters)

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 28, 2013 2:32 pm Gorbatschow wrote: 1. producing stereotypes (Rei is now a archetype)

Everybody knows this completely clichéd scene that appears nowadays in nearly every slightly romantic anime: One person ,of the pair with sexual or romantic tensions, stumbles or slips and lands on top of h
